message:
test(ftui-extras): improve spotlight.rs test coverage (bd-pl0rv)

Added 20 new tests (26→46 total) covering:
- SpotlightConfig: overlay_color/panel_bg/panel_fg/title_style builders, Debug+Clone
- Spotlight: default/Debug/Clone, config method
- PanelPosition: Debug+Clone+Copy+Eq
- padded_target: padding arithmetic, saturating_sub at edges, None case
- panel_size: title-only, narrow screen clamping, hints disabled
- grapheme_width ASCII path
- render_overlay: dims cells outside target, preserves target cutout
- Full render with all components, forced above position
- panel_bounds matches panel_size, panel clamped to screen bounds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3aa3","title":"Text Effects: Composable Effect Chains","description":"## Background
Current TextEffect enum is mutually exclusive - you can only have ONE effect. Users want combinations like 'rainbow AND pulsing'. This enables effect composition.

## Implementation

Change StyledText:
```rust
pub struct StyledText {
    text: String,
    effects: Vec<TextEffect>,  // Multiple effects, applied in order
    easing: Easing,            // Global easing for time-based effects
    // ... other fields
}
```

### Effect Categories and Combination Rules

Effects are categorized by what they modify:

| Category | Effects | Combination Rule |
|----------|---------|------------------|
| ColorModifier | Gradient, ColorCycle, ColorWave, Glow | BLEND: multiply/modulate colors |
| AlphaModifier | FadeIn, FadeOut, Pulse | MULTIPLY: alpha values multiply |
| PositionModifier | Wave, Bounce, Shake, Cascade | ADD: offsets sum together |
| CharModifier | Typewriter, Scramble, Glitch, Reveal | PRIORITY: first visible char wins |
| StyleModifier | bold/italic toggles | OR: flags combine |

### Blending Examples:
- Rainbow + Pulse: Rainbow provides hue, Pulse modulates brightness
- FadeIn + Pulse: Overall alpha = fade_alpha * pulse_alpha  
- Wave + Shake: Total offset = wave_offset + shake_offset
- Typewriter + Scramble: Revealed chars can be scrambled

### Builder API:
```rust
StyledText::new("Hello")
    .effect(TextEffect::RainbowGradient { speed: 0.1 })
    .effect(TextEffect::Pulse { speed: 2.0, min_alpha: 0.3 })
    .effect(TextEffect::Wave { amplitude: 2.0, wavelength: 10.0, speed: 1.0 })
    .easing(Easing::EaseInOut)

// Convenience for multiple effects at once
.effects([effect1, effect2, effect3])

// Clear all effects
.clear_effects()
```

### Performance Guard:
Maximum 8 effects per StyledText to prevent performance issues.

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_single_effect_backwards_compat: .effect(e) still works
- test_multiple_color_effects_blend: Rainbow + Pulse modulates
- test_multiple_alpha_effects_multiply: FadeIn * Pulse
- test_multiple_position_effects_add: Wave + Shake offsets sum
- test_char_effects_priority: First visible char wins
- test_effect_order_deterministic: Same effects = same output
- test_clear_effects: Returns to plain rendering
- test_empty_effects_vec: Renders as plain text
- test_max_effects_enforced: >8 effects errors or truncates

## Integration Tests
- Rainbow + Wave: Verify both color and position effects visible
- Pulse + Glow: Verify compound brightness looks correct

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Composable Effect Chains
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-4r5h","title":"Text Effects: Preset Library","description":"## Background
Users want great results without understanding individual effects. Presets provide curated, ready-to-use combinations that showcase the system's capabilities.

## Implementation

Add TextPreset enum:
```rust
pub enum TextPreset {
    // High-energy/modern
    Neon,       // Cyan/magenta wave, pulsing glow
    Cyberpunk,  // Glitch, chromatic aberration, animated gradient
    Matrix,     // Green cascade, fade trail, cyber chars
    
    // Classic/retro
    Retro,      // CRT scanlines, green phosphor, subtle flicker
    Typewriter, // Reveal by char with cursor block
    Terminal,   // Solid green, subtle pulse, no frills
    
    // Elegant/professional
    Cinematic,  // Slow fade, gold gradient, soft glow
    Elegant,    // Organic pulse, gold/cream gradient, drop shadow
    Minimal,    // Subtle fade, no color, clean
    
    // Dynamic/playful
    Fire,       // Fire gradient, upward wave motion
    Ice,        // Frost blue gradient, sparkle effect
    Hologram,   // Rainbow shift, scanlines, transparency pulse
}
```

### Preset Specifications

| Preset | Effects | Colors | Special |
|--------|---------|--------|---------|
| Neon | Wave, PulsingGlow, ColorWave | Cyan/Magenta | Bold text |
| Cyberpunk | Glitch, ChromaticAberration, AnimatedGradient | Cyberpunk palette | Occasional matrix chars |
| Matrix | Cascade, FadeOut trail, Glitch | Green shades | CyberChars |
| Retro | Scanline, ColorCycle, Glitch(0.02) | Terminal green | Phosphor glow |
| Typewriter | Reveal(LeftToRight), cursor | White | Monospace feel |
| Terminal | Pulse(subtle) | Green | Simple |
| Cinematic | FadeIn(eased), HorizontalGradient, Glow | Gold/White | Drop shadow |
| Elegant | OrganicPulse, Gradient, Shadow | Gold/Cream | Italic option |
| Minimal | FadeIn | Theme default | No decoration |
| Fire | AnimatedGradient(fire), Wave(up) | Fire palette | Bold |
| Ice | Gradient(ice), Sparkle, Pulse | Blue/White | - |
| Hologram | RainbowGradient, Scanline, Pulse | Rainbow | Transparency |

### Builder API:
```rust
// Apply preset
StyledText::preset(TextPreset::Neon)

// Customize after preset
StyledText::preset(TextPreset::Neon)
    .base_color(custom_cyan)  // Override default
    .easing(Easing::Bounce)   // Change animation feel

// Get preset effects for inspection
let effects = TextPreset::Neon.effects();
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_preset_applies_effects: Each preset sets non-empty effect list
- test_preset_customizable: base_color override works
- test_preset_easing_override: Can change easing after preset
- test_all_presets_render: No panic for any preset at t=0, t=0.5, t=1
- test_preset_effects_consistent: Same preset = same effects
- test_preset_no_conflicts: No preset has conflicting effects

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot all 12 presets at t=0.5
- Verify each preset is visually distinct

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Preset Library
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-4yonz","title":"perf(framebuffer): stride==1 blit full-coverage fast path","description":"Single-lever optimization in DoomFramebuffer::blit_to_painter and QuakeFramebuffer::blit_to_painter: when stride==1 (full coverage), call painter.mark_full_coverage() once and use set_color_at_index_in_bounds for each pixel to avoid per-pixel generation-stamp overhead. Keep sparse stride path using point_colored_at_index_in_bounds. Validate with fmt/check/clippy + framebuffer tests + VFX harness doom/quak.","notes":"Blocked on PurpleCreek exclusive reservations (doom/quak framebuffer + renderers). I validated the current shared-tree diff and posted gates + harness deltas in Agent Mail thread bd-4yonz; ready to land once reservations are released.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:05:03.317001055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:15:20.227587749Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:15:20.227567772Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doom","ftui-extras","perf","quake"],"comments":[{"id":1143,"issue_id":"bd-4yonz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LavenderDesert (2026-02-10): Implemented stride==1 full-coverage fast path in both DoomFramebuffer::blit_to_painter and QuakeFramebuffer::blit_to_painter. Commit c25ab0c6. Validation: 103 doom/quake framebuffer tests pass, golden hash registry (5 scenarios) all pass — output bit-identical. Changes: when stride==1, call painter.mark_full_coverage() then use set_color_at_index_in_bounds (skipping per-pixel generation-stamp overhead). When stride>1, unchanged path using point_colored_at_index_in_bounds.","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:07:49Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-4yonz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LavenderDesert (2026-02-10): Clean-worktree A/B benchmark (commit 8a2bb9e9 vs c25ab0c6, release build, 120x40, 220 frames, seed=42):

Doom E1M1:
  BEFORE total_p95=0.948ms render_p95=0.892ms
  AFTER  total_p95=0.621ms render_p95=0.597ms
  Delta: total -34.49%, render -33.07%

Quake E1M1:
  BEFORE total_p95=1.448ms render_p95=1.427ms
  AFTER  total_p95=1.462ms render_p95=1.438ms
  Delta: total +0.97% (noise), render +0.77% (noise)

The mark_full_coverage + set_color_at_index_in_bounds fast path delivers a substantial 34% win for Doom where blit is a larger fraction of frame time. Quake sees no measurable change because BSP rendering dominates. Golden hash registry passes for all 5 effects.","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:15:19Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-6e9","title":"Bubbles Widget Collection (charmed_rust port)","description":"# Bubbles Widget Collection (optional, feature-gated)

## Purpose
This epic tracks additional widgets inspired by `charmed_rust` (Bubbletea/Bubbles ecosystem).

These are **not required** for the core ftui kernel or the first agent harness deliverable, but we keep them as optional feature-gated extensions.

## Reference Source
- `legacy_reference_library_code/charmed_rust/`

## Plan Alignment
The plan explicitly de-scopes “widget zoo” into Phase 7:
- ship kernel + runtime + harness first
- only then add extra widgets

## Scope
Widgets here must:
- render deterministically
- respect focus/input conventions
- avoid terminal side effects (all output goes through ftui’s one-writer routing)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Widgets are feature-gated and cannot affect kernel invariants when disabled.
- [ ] Each widget has snapshot tests + interaction/unit tests where applicable.
- [ ] Widgets integrate with ftui-style/theme and ftui-runtime subscriptions cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:57:47.683736546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:01:40.867306738Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:01:40.867228872Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6e9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":485,"issue_id":"bd-6e9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ouql","title":"Emoji/grapheme width correctness across text/render/extras","description":"Background: emoji and ZWJ sequences render as width=2 in most terminals; current width logic uses unicode-width with unwrap_or(0/1) and misses emoji width and zero-width codepoints. Goal: standardize grapheme/display width across ftui-text, ftui-render, and ftui-extras using a single, accurate width model, with zero-width codepoint handling and emoji-aware width=2. Scope: ftui-text wrap/segment/width_cache/tests, ftui-render drawing/frame, ftui-extras widgets relying on grapheme width. Constraints: deterministic, no unsafe. Tests: update corpus tests + add explicit emoji/ZWJ/VS cases, proptests for control/zero-width, ensure benchmarks use new width. Acceptance: corpus passes, emojis width=2, combining/ZWJ/VS width=0 when standalone; no regressions in render; large-screen and inline/alt-screen behavior unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T06:04:12.780592405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:14:05.189638438Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T06:14:05.189618861Z","close_reason":"Completed: emoji-aware width (unicode-display-width) with control/tab handling + tests updated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-pt0dv","title":"Mermaid Parser Core (Tokenizer + AST)","description":"Background\n- We must parse Mermaid fenced blocks into a deterministic AST.\n- Mermaid syntax varies by diagram type; a shared tokenizer + AST core prevents drift.\n\nScope\n- Implement tokenizer: keywords, identifiers, arrows, brackets, directives, strings, comments.\n- Parse Mermaid init directives (%%{init: {...}}%%) into AST nodes.\n- Parse comments/annotations and ignore or retain for diagnostics.\n- Build AST for all supported diagram types (flowchart, sequence, state, gantt, class, ER, mindmap, pie).\n- Parse subgraph/cluster blocks and diagram direction directives.\n- Parse styling directives (classDef/class/style/linkStyle) as AST nodes.\n- Parse link/click directives into metadata (even if degraded at render).\n- Robust error reporting with source spans (line/col) and expected tokens.\n- Error recovery: resynchronize at line boundaries to continue AST build when possible.\n- Deterministic normalization of whitespace + line endings.\n\nConsiderations\n- Avoid parser generators unless already used; keep dependencies minimal.\n- Avoid allocations in hot loops (use slices/indices).\n- Parser must never panic on malformed input.\n\nTests / Validation\n- Unit tests for lexing + parsing per diagram type.\n- Golden AST fixtures for canonical samples.\n- Error-case tests asserting span + error kind.\n- Seeded fuzz tests for tokenizer + parser to ensure no panics.\n\nSuccess Criteria\n- AST captures nodes, edges, labels, directives, and diagram type.\n- Parser errors are structured and location-aware; recovery yields partial AST when possible.\n- Parser returns identical AST for semantically equivalent whitespace.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyCompass","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:38:02.271400086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:25:39.069074956Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:25:39.069053856Z","close_reason":"Mermaid core parser: directives/comments/subgraph/style/link + error recovery + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ptyoh","title":"Fix demo showcase mouse navigation + hit regions","description":"Purpose:
- Restore intuitive mouse interactions across the demo showcase, especially menu section clicks that used to work.

Background:
- User reports menu section clicks no longer navigate; mouse should do the obvious across the entire demo showcase.

Scope:
- Audit hit‑region registration for menu items and screen lists.
- Verify mouse event routing (click, hover, scroll) in showcase runtime and ensure it maps to the correct HitId.
- Fix regression so left‑click selects/activates the intended section/screen.
- Ensure hover highlights and click activation align with current layout/scroll offsets.

Testing:
- Add unit tests for hit‑region registration mapping to menu items.
- Add E2E test that simulates mouse clicks on menu sections and verifies navigation.
- Add JSONL logging for mouse events: event type, x/y, hit id, resolved action.

Acceptance:
- Clicking any menu section navigates reliably.
- Mouse interactions feel intuitive across the demo showcase.
- Tests + logs prevent regression.
","notes":"Added PTY-driven mouse tab click E2E test (SGR mouse sequences) in terminal_e2e; JSONL switch_screen assertion. Ran fmt/check/clippy and targeted test. Fixed clippy warnings in mermaid.rs and command_palette; cleaned screen_snapshots imports.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:00:16.592842662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:16:22.921206675Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:16:22.921188030Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
